findings.summary: Nimbolide, the principal bioactive limonoid of Azadirachta indica, inhibits P-glycoprotein (P-gp/ABCB1) in vitro, reducing methotrexate efflux from cells and increasing intracellular drug accumulation; since P-gp mediates renal tubular secretion of methotrexate, its inhibition can cause clinically significant drug accumulation and toxicity.
findings.citation: Suresh et al., Phytomedicine 2017; transporter inhibition data

findings.summary: Both methotrexate and neem leaf extract are independently hepatotoxic: methotrexate causes dose-cumulative hepatic fibrosis, while nimbolide and azadirachtin at high doses produce hepatocellular injury in animal models; co-administration raises serious concern for additive hepatotoxicity in rheumatoid arthritis patients.
findings.citation: Chattopadhyay, Curr Sci 2011; methotrexate hepatotoxicity review

findings.summary: A clinical report describes a patient with rheumatoid arthritis on methotrexate 15 mg/week who developed elevated liver enzymes and pancytopenia after adding neem leaf capsules for 6 weeks; enzyme normalisation occurred 8 weeks after neem discontinuation without methotrexate dose change.
